headline: Tate & Lyle Accepts Bid From Ingredion in Loss for London
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), in a move that marks the end of the UK company’s near-century on the London Stock Exchange.Most Read from BloombergHouse Republican Says Hegseth’s D-Day Remarks ‘Inappropriate’Trump Says He, Not Congress, Is in Charge of Kennedy Center in ReversalWhy Oil’s Not at $200 After the Biggest Supply Shock in HistoryIsrael Strikes Iran After Missile Attack, Imperiling Trump TalksAI Trade Unwind Rocks Korean Trad

content.title: Tate & Lyle Accepts Bid From Ingredion in Loss for London
content.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), in a move that marks the end of the UK company’s near-century on the London Stock Exchange.Most Read from BloombergHouse Republican Says Hegseth’s D-Day Remarks ‘Inappropriate’Trump Says He, Not Congress, Is in Charge of Kennedy Center in ReversalWhy Oil’s Not at $200 After the Biggest Supply Shock in HistoryIsrael Strikes Iran After Missile Attack, Imperiling Trump TalksAI Trade Unwind Rocks Korean Trad

headline: Further ~2% downside in Nasdaq could trigger broader CTA unwinds, BofA warns
summary: Investing.com -- Friday's sharp Nasdaq selloff has likely begun a more coordinated unwind in systematic equity positioning, and another 2% decline could trigger broader deleveraging, Bank of America warned.

content.title: Further ~2% downside in Nasdaq could trigger broader CTA unwinds, BofA warns
